fields.title: TechTribe Social Media App
fields.short_description: A Django-based social media platform with user profiles, posts, friend requests, and a friends-only feed.
fields.description:
TechTribe is a full-stack social media web application built with Django. Users can register and create personal profiles, publish posts, send and accept friend requests, and browse a personalised feed showing content only from their friends.

The project demonstrates end-to-end web development skills — from database design and back-end logic to front-end styling — and was deployed to Heroku with a PostgreSQL production database.
fields.tools_used: Python, Django, HTML, CSS, Bootstrap, SQLite, PostgreSQL, Heroku
fields.outcome:
Delivered a fully functional social networking platform with core features including:
- User registration, authentication, and profile management
- Post creation and friends-only feed
- Friend request system (send, accept, decline)
- Responsive UI built with Bootstrap
- Deployed live on Heroku with SQLite.
fields.business_problem: There was a need to demonstrate end-to-end full-stack web development capability — building a social networking product from scratch, covering authentication, relational data modelling, dynamic feeds, and cloud deployment — while producing a working, deployable application rather than a prototype.
fields.key_features:
- User registration, authentication, and profile management
- Post creation and a friends-only personalised feed
- Friend request system — send, accept, and decline requests
- Relational data model using Django ORM with PostgreSQL
- Responsive UI built with Bootstrap 5
- Deployed live on Heroku with a production PostgreSQL database
fields.role_contribution: Sole developer responsible for the full project lifecycle — from database schema design and Django back-end development to front-end templating, Bootstrap styling, and production deployment on Heroku. Designed all models, views, URL routing, and template logic independently.
fields.biggest_challenge:
- Designing and managing relational database models for users, posts, profiles, friendships, and friend requests while maintaining clean relationships between tables.
- Implementing authentication and authorization securely using Django’s built-in user management system.
- Building a dynamic friends-only feed that displayed posts correctly based on accepted friendship relationships.
- Developing the friend request workflow logic to properly handle sending, accepting, declining, and preventing duplicate requests.
- Debugging Django routing, template rendering, and view logic during development.
- Handling deployment challenges on Heroku, including environment variables, static files configuration, database setup, and dependency management.
-Managing Git and version control while preventing sensitive files such as .env and virtual environments from being pushed to the repository.
fields.what_you_learned:
- How to build a complete full-stack web application using Django from database design to deployment.
- Practical experience with the MVC/MVT architecture and how Django handles models, views, templates, and routing.
- How relational databases work in real-world applications, including foreign keys, one-to-one relationships, and many-to-many relationships.
- Improved Python and Django development skills through implementing authentication, CRUD operations, and dynamic user interactions.
- How to build personalized content feeds and implement user-based access control logic.
- Hands-on experience with front-end integration using HTML, CSS, and Bootstrap for responsive UI design.
- How to deploy and manage cloud-hosted applications, including configuring static files, environment variables, and production settings.
- Greater understanding of end-to-end software development lifecycle processes, from planning and development to deployment and maintenance.

fields.title: Oracle AML — Payment System Integration
fields.short_description: Led the end-to-end integration of Oracle Anti-Money Laundering (AML) with the bank's payments and account-opening platforms to identify high-risk individuals and suspicious payment behaviors during onboarding.
fields.description:
Led the end-to-end integration of Oracle Anti-Money Laundering (AML) with the bank's payments and account-opening platforms to identify high-risk individuals and suspicious payment behaviors during onboarding. The integration enabled faster detection of risky applicants, reduced false negatives, and provided a documented audit trail for regulatory reviewers.

 Role & Responsibilities

- Acted as liaison between Compliance, IT, and Oracle vendor teams.
- Elicited and documented AML integration and compliance requirements.
- Mapped customer and transaction data fields between systems.
- Coordinated design, development, testing, and deployment activities.
- Oversaw UAT and production rollout ensuring readiness of compliance teams.
- Implemented KPI tracking to measure fraud detection and false positive rates.

Business Objectives & Success Criteria

- Comply with AML and KYC regulations.
- Identify high-risk customers during onboarding to prevent fraud and sanctions breaches.
- Reduce detection time from 24 hours to near real-time.
- Minimize false positive rates (target <10%).
- Provide traceable audit logs for regulators and auditors.
fields.tools_used: JIRA (backlog and sprints), Confluence (documentation), Postman (API testing), SQL & Python for data validation, Splunk/ELK for logs and monitoring, Git for source control. Regular demos and compliance sign-offs after each milestone ensured alignment.
fields.outcome: Delivered a real-time AML integration that reduced false positives by 50%, improved detection speed from 24 hours to near real-time, and strengthened regulatory compliance through automated audit trails.
fields.business_problem: The bank's onboarding and payments systems operated in silos with no real-time connection to AML screening. High-risk applicants and suspicious transactions were only detected through slow batch processes (up to 24 hours after the fact), creating significant regulatory exposure, compliance gaps, and potential for sanctions breaches. The manual investigation process was resource-intensive and lacked a structured audit trail required by regulators.
fields.key_features:
Real-time data feeds from onboarding and payments systems into Oracle AML
Customer identity resolution and watchlist (PEP/Sanctions) screening at onboarding
Automated case creation workflows with investigator notifications for high-risk matches
Standardised decision outcomes: PASS, ALERT, HOLD, and BLOCK
Operational compliance dashboards and audit-ready reporting for regulators
KPI tracking for fraud detection accuracy and false positive rates
Runbooks, training materials, and 4-week hypercare support post-launch
fields.role_contribution: Acted as the lead Business Analyst and Integration Coordinator across Compliance, IT, and the Oracle vendor team. Elicited and documented all AML integration requirements, mapped customer and transaction data fields between source and target systems, and defined the rule taxonomy and decisioning logic with the Compliance team. Coordinated design, development, testing, and UAT, and oversaw the production rollout and hypercare period.

fields.title: ATM Channel Enhancement & Self-Service Kiosk Implementation
fields.short_description: Led the ATM Channel Enhancement and Self-Service Kiosk Integration project as a Technical Business Analyst and Project Manager, supporting Access Bank Plc's digital transformation initiative. Coordinated requirements gathering, vendor collaboration (NCR, Interswitch, Oracle), and system integration
fields.description:
As part of the bank's digital transformation initiative, I led the project to enhance the functionality of ATMs and deploy self-service kiosks across select branches. The primary goal was to reduce customer queues and in-branch foot traffic by enabling customers to perform more banking transactions through self-service channels. The initiative aimed to modernize customer experience, optimize branch operations, and strengthen the bank's omnichannel service delivery.

Access Bank initiated the ATM Channel Enhancement and Self-Service Kiosk Integration project to reduce branch foot traffic by extending more banking services to self-service channels.

As the Technical Business Analyst and Project Manager, I led the requirements gathering, system integration with Oracle FLEXCUBE, vendor coordination (NCR, Interswitch, Oracle), and deployment of self-service kiosks across multiple branches.

The project enabled customers to perform account updates, card requests, interbank transfers, bill payments, and cheque deposits through ATMs and kiosks — reducing in-branch transactions by 45% within six months.
fields.tools_used: JIRA, Confluence, MS Teams, Oracle FLEXCUBE 14.x, Splunk, Oracle Enterprise Manager, In-house .NET-based app, NCR ATM (via Interswitch Switch)
fields.outcome:
The ATM and Self-Service Kiosk Integration Project modernized the Bank's retail operations and supported its long-term digital transformation roadmap.

By combining strong business analysis, agile project execution, and technical integration with Oracle FLEXCUBE, the initiative demonstrated how technology can drive operational efficiency and customer empowerment in the financial services sector there by reducing footfall at the branches country wide.
fields.business_problem: Branch congestion was a major operational challenge for Access Bank. Customers visited branches for routine services — account updates, card reissuance, and interbank transfers — resulting in long queues, poor customer satisfaction scores, inefficient use of teller resources, and higher operational costs per transaction. The bank needed to offload these transactions to automated self-service channels integrated directly with its core banking system.
fields.key_features:
ATM enhancements: interbank transfers, utility bill payments, mini-statement, cheque book request, and address update
Self-service kiosks: instant card printing, biometric/KYC update, cheque deposit with image scanning, CRM-linked service request ticketing
Middleware integration between NCR ATMs, Interswitch, and Oracle FLEXCUBE via ISO 8583 and SOAP/REST APIs
Security layer: end-to-end encryption, tokenisation, and two-factor authentication
Splunk dashboards for real-time transaction monitoring and SLA tracking
UAT across 5 pilot branches before full nationwide rollout
fields.role_contribution: Served as both Technical Business Analyst and Project Manager. Led stakeholder workshops with Digital Banking, IT Infrastructure, and Operations teams. Produced the Functional Requirements Document (FRD), System Requirements Specification (SRS), and use case definitions. Managed vendor coordination with NCR, Interswitch, and Oracle. Developed the project plan, risk register, and communications matrix. Oversaw integration testing, UAT, and pilot rollout across 10 high-traffic branches, and provided executive reporting to the Head of Channels.

fields.title: Expatriate Tracking System (ETS) Automation
fields.short_description: Led the design and implementation of a centralized Expatriate Tracking System (ETS) to digitize and automate workforce compliance and offshore mobilization processes. The platform replaced fragmented Excel-based tracking with a web-based solution .
fields.description:
Problem Overview (Before Automation)

The company relied heavily on Excel sheets, emails, and manual follow-ups to track expatriates' documents and mobilization status. This created serious operational and compliance risks:

Key Issues:

- Visas, work permits, and Safety cards expired without warning.
- HR, HSE, and Operations used different spreadsheets, causing data inconsistency.
- Mobilizations were delayed because medicals or training certificates weren't validated on time.
- Preparing compliance reports for internal or client audits took 2–3 days.
- Offshore rotation schedules didn't link to document validity, creating last-minute delays.

This manual process was not scalable for a company with expatriates deployed across multiple offshore locations.
fields.tools_used: Jira, Confluence, Figma, Draw.io, Postman, Power BI, Azure DevOps, SharePoint/Teams
fields.outcome:
After implementation:

- 90% reduction in permit-expiry incidents
- 35% improvement in mobilization processing times
- Audit preparation time reduced from 3 days to same-day
- Zero missed medical/training renewals during the pilot phase
- Clear accountability through full audit trails
fields.business_problem: SeaAvin relied entirely on Excel spreadsheets, emails, and manual follow-ups to track expatriate documents and mobilization status across multiple offshore locations. Visas and work permits expired without warning, HR and Operations maintained inconsistent data in separate spreadsheets, mobilisations were frequently delayed due to unvalidated medicals or training certificates, and audit preparation took 2–3 days. The process was unscalable and created serious regulatory and safety compliance risks in a high-risk oil and gas environment.
fields.key_features:
Centralised expatriate profile management with document upload and verification
Automated expiry alerts and escalation workflows for visas, work permits, and safety certifications
Offshore/onshore rotation planning linked directly to document validity status
HSE training and medical clearance validation workflow
Mobilisation approval gate — clearance only granted when all conditions are met
Automated Gate Pass and Site Access List generation for client sites
Real-time compliance dashboards and same-day audit-ready reporting
Role-based access control for HR, HSE, Operations, and management
fields.role_contribution: Led the end-to-end business analysis and project management for the ETS implementation. Conducted stakeholder workshops with HR, HSE, and Operations to elicit requirements and map current-state processes. Defined the system scope, produced process flow diagrams in Draw.io, and authored the FRD and user stories in Confluence. Managed the development backlog in Azure DevOps, coordinated UAT, and oversaw pilot deployment and user training.
fields.biggest_challenge:
- Consolidating fragmented expatriate data spread across multiple Excel sheets, emails, and departmental records into a centralized system.
- Designing workflows that aligned HR, HSE, Operations, and compliance processes without disrupting existing offshore operations.
- Managing complex document lifecycles for visas, work permits, medicals, safety certifications, and training renewals with different expiration timelines.
- Building automated alert and escalation mechanisms to prevent missed renewals and mobilization delays.
- Integrating offshore rotation schedules with document validity rules to ensure only compliant personnel were cleared for deployment.
- Ensuring data accuracy and consistency while migrating historical records from manual spreadsheets into the new system.
- Developing audit-ready reporting capabilities that could generate accurate compliance reports quickly for internal reviews and client audits.
- Handling role-based access and accountability requirements so departments could update relevant information while maintaining security and traceability.
- Overcoming resistance to process change from teams accustomed to manual tracking methods and spreadsheet-based workflows.
fields.what_you_learned:
- How to analyze and redesign inefficient operational processes using workflow automation and centralized data management.
- Practical experience implementing business process automation for compliance-heavy operational environments.
- Improved understanding of document lifecycle management, regulatory compliance tracking, and offshore workforce mobilization processes.
- How to design systems that integrate multiple business functions including HR, HSE, Operations, and compliance management.
- The importance of real-time visibility, automated alerts, and audit trails in reducing operational risk and improving accountability.
- Hands-on experience designing role-based workflows and approval processes for enterprise operational systems.
- How automation can significantly reduce manual administrative workload while improving data accuracy and operational speed.
- Better understanding of reporting and dashboard requirements for audit preparation, compliance monitoring, and executive visibility.

fields.title: n8n Multi-Agent Workflow — Handyman Ops Desk
fields.short_description: AI-powered n8n Cloud automation that processes handyman job requests end-to-end: intake parsing with Gemini, rules-based pricing, slot scheduling, and dual-path email dispatch.
fields.description:
This project is a beginner-to-intermediate multi-agent automation built in n8n Cloud using the drag-and-drop GUI — no server setup required. The Orchestrator workflow chains four specialised agent workflows via live webhook HTTP calls, processing a handyman service request from start to finish.

Workflow Architecture:

Agent 1 — Intake: Receives a free-text customer description from a Google Sheets form trigger and sends it to Google Gemini 2.5 Flash, which extracts structured data — service type, address, zip code, urgency level, preferred days, and diagnostic notes — returning clean JSON.

Agent 2 — Pricing: Applies JavaScript rules against a service catalog (faucet replace, ceiling fan, TV mount, garbage disposal). Adjusts for zip-code-based travel costs and adds a 20% rush surcharge for high-urgency jobs. Returns an itemised estimate in USD.

Agent 3 — Scheduling: Queries a live time API (timeapi.io) for the current Chicago timezone, then filters a pool of available slots against the customer's preferred days, returning up to three proposed appointment times.

Agent 4 — Comms: Passes the collated job, pricing, and scheduling data to Gemini, which drafts a friendly, under-120-word confirmation message personalised to the customer.

Orchestrator — Handyman Ops Desk: Triggered by new rows in a Google Sheets form response sheet. Calls each agent in sequence, merges results, then routes through a conditional branch — high-urgency jobs or estimates over $300 trigger an operations alert email; all other jobs receive a client-facing confirmation email via Gmail.
fields.tools_used: n8n Cloud, Google Gemini 2.5 Flash, Google Sheets, Gmail, JavaScript, HTTP Webhooks, timeapi.io
fields.outcome:
Fully automated end-to-end job intake pipeline — from Google Forms submission to dual-path email dispatch — with zero manual intervention required.

Key results:
- AI-structured intake eliminates manual data entry and misclassification errors
- Rules-based pricing engine delivers consistent, transparent estimates with dynamic urgency surcharges
- Preference-aware scheduling returns relevant appointment slots instantly
- Conditional routing ensures high-priority jobs are escalated to operations while standard jobs receive an immediate client confirmation
- All five workflows are modular and independently testable, making them reusable across other service verticals
fields.business_problem: A handyman business was handling all job intake, pricing, and scheduling manually — relying on phone calls, emails, and spreadsheets. This was time-consuming, inconsistent, and impossible to scale. High-urgency jobs were not being escalated promptly, pricing was applied inconsistently, and customers experienced delays in receiving booking confirmations.
fields.key_features:
AI-powered job intake using Gemini 2.5 Flash to extract structured data from free-text customer requests
Rules-based pricing engine with zip-code travel adjustment and 20% rush surcharge for high-urgency jobs
Preference-aware scheduling that filters available slots against customer's preferred days
AI-generated personalised confirmation message under 120 words
Conditional routing: high-urgency or high-value jobs trigger an operations alert; all others get a client confirmation
Five modular, independently testable n8n workflows linked via HTTP webhooks
fields.role_contribution: Designed and built all five n8n Cloud workflows from scratch — Intake, Pricing, Scheduling, Comms, and Orchestrator. Configured the Gemini AI integration for structured data extraction and message generation. Implemented the JavaScript pricing rules engine with dynamic surcharge logic. Designed the conditional routing logic and end-to-end Google Sheets-to-Gmail pipeline. No server setup or coding environment required — entirely delivered through the n8n visual interface.
fields.biggest_challenge:
- Designing reliable communication between workflows using webhook HTTP calls without breaking the data flow between agents.
- Ensuring scheduling logic returned realistic appointment slots that matched customer preferences while accounting for real-time date and time validation.
-Debugging multi-step automations in n8n Cloud, where one failed node could interrupt the entire orchestration pipeline.
fields.what_you_learned:
- How to design and orchestrate multi-agent workflow architectures using n8n Cloud.
- Practical experience integrating AI models such as Google Gemini into automation pipelines for structured data extraction and natural language generation.
- How to use webhook-based communication to connect modular workflows into a scalable automation system.
- How to design scalable automation systems that reduce manual operational workload while improving response speed and consistency.
- Real-world exposure to process automation, workflow optimization, and operational reengineering concepts commonly used in modern IT and business operations roles.

fields.title: Campus SkillSwap — Student Skill Marketplace
fields.short_description: Full-stack Django marketplace where students post, discover, and book peer skills — built with GitHub Copilot using a Vibe Coding workflow in VS Code.
fields.description:
Campus SkillSwap is a full-stack Django web application built as a student peer-to-peer marketplace where users can post, browse, and book skills or services offered by other students.

The project was built using Vibe Coding — an AI-assisted development workflow in VS Code using GitHub Copilot — demonstrating the ability to rapidly scaffold, extend, and customise a production-ready Django application with AI pair programming.

Core Features:

User Authentication — Full registration, login, and logout system using Django's built-in auth framework. Each user gets a personal dashboard showing their posted skills and incoming booking requests.

Skill Listings — Students can create, edit, and delete skill posts with fields for title, description, category, price (or free), contact preference, and availability status.

Category Filtering — Browse page supports filtering by category with visual badge indicators on each card.

Search — Simple keyword search across skill titles and categories.

Review & Rating System — Users can leave star ratings and written reviews on skills they have used.

Booking / Appointments System — Students can submit session requests directly from a skill detail page. The skill owner receives and manages incoming requests through their dashboard.
fields.tools_used: Django, Python, Bootstrap, SQLite, GitHub Copilot, HTML, CSS, JavaScript
fields.outcome:
Successfully delivered a fully functional multi-user marketplace application with five core feature areas:

- Authentication system with per-user dashboards and skill ownership
- CRUD skill listings with category taxonomy and availability management
- Peer review and star rating system for trust and credibility
- Booking and appointments workflow covering request submission through to scheduling
- Polished custom UI with dark theme, gradient branding, and responsive card grid

The project demonstrates end-to-end Django development — models, views, forms, templates, URL routing, and admin — as well as practical experience with AI-assisted development (GitHub Copilot / Vibe Coding) to accelerate delivery without sacrificing code quality or learning outcomes.
fields.business_problem: Students on campus had no dedicated platform to discover or offer peer skills and services. Informal exchanges happened through group chats and word of mouth, making it difficult to find the right person, agree on terms, or build trust. There was a need for a structured marketplace where students could post skills, receive bookings, and build credibility through peer reviews.
fields.key_features:
User registration, login, and personal dashboard with skill ownership
CRUD skill listings with category, price, availability status, and contact preference
Category filtering and keyword search across all listings
Peer review and star rating system to build trust between students
Booking and appointments workflow from request submission through to scheduling
Admin panel for full content management
Custom dark-themed responsive UI with Bootstrap 5
fields.role_contribution: Sole developer using an AI-assisted Vibe Coding workflow in VS Code with GitHub Copilot. Designed the full data model, implemented all Django views, forms, URL routing, and template logic, and built the custom UI from scratch. Used Copilot for code scaffolding and autocompletion, making independent decisions on architecture, feature scope, and UX design throughout.
fields.biggest_challenge:
- Designing and managing relational database models for users, skills, bookings, reviews, and ratings while maintaining clean relationships between application components.
- Implementing secure authentication and authorization so users could only manage their own skills, bookings, and dashboard content.
- Building a complete booking workflow that handled request submission, appointment management, and dashboard notifications smoothly.
- Creating dynamic search and category filtering features that returned accurate and relevant results.
- Developing the peer review and star rating system while preventing duplicate or invalid reviews.
- Maintaining consistency between front-end templates, forms, views, and database models across multiple interconnected features.
- Creating a polished custom UI with responsive layouts, dark theme styling, and gradient branding.
- Using Vibe Coding effectively to rapidly build features while still validating the generated logic and ensuring the application architecture remained maintainable.
- Debugging AI-generated Django code suggestions that occasionally introduced template errors, broken routes, inefficient queries, or incorrect model relationships.
- Balancing rapid AI-assisted development with understanding the underlying Django concepts and application flow.
fields.what_you_learned:
- How to build a complete full-stack marketplace application using Django, including models, views, templates, forms, URL routing, and authentication.
- Practical experience building multi-user systems with role-based ownership, relational data models, and personalized dashboards.
- How peer-to-peer marketplace platforms function, including listings, booking systems, review workflows, and trust-building mechanisms.
- Improved front-end development skills using HTML, CSS, Bootstrap, and custom responsive UI design.
- How to integrate search, category filtering, and dynamic content rendering into a Django application.
- Hands-on experience implementing CRUD operations and managing complex application state across multiple features.
- How to use Vibe Coding and AI-assisted development workflows productively to accelerate feature development and prototyping.
- Better understanding of modern AI pair-programming workflows using tools like GitHub Copilot inside Visual Studio Code.

fields.title: Handyman AI Assistant — LangChain + Gemini
fields.short_description: LangChain agent powered by Gemini 2.5 Flash that handles handyman job enquiries, pulls live pricing from Google Sheets, and books appointments directly into Google Calendar.
fields.description:
Handyman AI Project is a LangChain-powered conversational AI assistant built to automate service booking and pricing for a handyman business. The project integrates Google's Gemini 2.5 Flash model with real-world business tools — Google Sheets for dynamic pricing and Google Calendar for availability and appointment booking.

The project was built by cloning a starter repository and fully customising it to connect to personal cloud resources, demonstrating end-to-end AI agent development with external tool integrations.

Technical Stack:

LangChain + LangGraph — agent orchestration framework managing tool calls, memory, and conversation flow across multiple turns.

Google Gemini 2.5 Flash via langchain-google-genai — the core LLM powering natural language understanding, intent detection, and response generation.

Google Sheets Integration — the agent queries a live Google Sheet to look up service pricing dynamically based on the job type requested by the customer.

Google Calendar Integration — the agent checks Google Calendar availability and creates booking entries directly from the conversation, removing manual scheduling.
fields.tools_used: LangChain, LangGraph, Google Gemini 2.5 Flash, Google Sheets API, Google Calendar API, Python, LangSmith, python-dotenv
fields.outcome:
Successfully deployed a working AI assistant that handles a full customer interaction cycle — from describing a service need, receiving a price estimate pulled live from Google Sheets, to having an appointment booked directly into Google Calendar.

Key outcomes:
- End-to-end LangChain agent with real external tool integrations (not mocked)
- Live Google Sheets pricing lookup replaces static price lists
- Automated Google Calendar booking eliminates manual appointment entry
- Multi-turn conversation with chat history preserves context across the session
- Demonstrates practical LLM deployment: prompt engineering, tool binding, and agent loop design
fields.business_problem: Handyman businesses spend a significant portion of their time manually answering enquiries, quoting prices, and coordinating appointments — tasks that are repetitive, time-consuming, and error-prone. There was an opportunity to deploy a conversational AI agent that could handle the full customer interaction cycle autonomously, from understanding the service need to booking the appointment, without any manual intervention.
fields.key_features:
Multi-turn conversational AI with persistent chat history using LangChain + LangGraph
Live Google Sheets pricing lookup — no static price lists, always current
Google Calendar availability check and direct appointment booking from within the conversation
Two chatbot versions: v1 (single-turn) and v2 (full multi-turn with graceful exit)
LangSmith tracing and observability for agent monitoring
pytest + pytest-asyncio test suite for agent behaviour validation
fields.role_contribution: Cloned a starter LangChain repository and fully customised the agent to connect to personal Google cloud resources (Sheets and Calendar). Configured the Gemini 2.5 Flash LLM via langchain-google-genai, defined and bound the Google Sheets and Calendar tools, built both v1 and v2 chatbot versions, set up LangSmith for observability, and wrote the pytest test suite. Managed all credentials and environment configuration via python-dotenv.
fields.biggest_challenge:
- Integrating multiple external services including Google Sheets and Google Calendar into a single conversational AI workflow while maintaining reliable communication between tools.
- Managing authentication, API credentials, OAuth permissions, and secure access to cloud resources during setup and deployment.
- Designing a conversational flow that could accurately interpret user intent, maintain context across multiple turns, and decide when to trigger external tools.
- Building reliable LangChain agent logic that correctly selected tools for pricing lookup versus appointment scheduling.
- Handling inconsistent or vague customer inputs while still returning useful pricing estimates and booking options.
- Debugging agent loops, memory handling, and tool-calling behavior inside LangChain and LangGraph workflows.
fields.what_you_learned:
- How to build and customize a real-world conversational AI application using LangChain and - LangGraph for agent orchestration and tool management.
Practical experience integrating LLMs with external APIs and cloud-based business tools.
- How to use Google Gemini models for intent detection, conversational reasoning, and response generation in business automation scenarios.
- Hands-on experience implementing tool binding, agent loops, memory management, and multi-step conversational workflows.
- How AI agents can automate repetitive business operations such as customer enquiries, pricing, and appointment scheduling.
- Improved understanding of prompt engineering and how prompts influence tool usage, reasoning flow, and conversational accuracy.

fields.title: Video Generation with Nano Banana — A Mother's Grief
fields.short_description: AI-generated 21-second short film using Nano Banana — a grieving mother confronts her gang-member son in a fully prompted, emotionally driven narrative scene.
fields.description:
Ingredients to Video is an AI creative project that uses generative AI tools to produce a short emotional film from scratch — starting with a written scene concept and ending with a fully animated, narrative-driven video.

The Scene:

A grieving mother confronts her only son after losing her husband. She discovers he has joined a gang, and in tears asks him why he is trying to destroy her when he is all she has left. Her son breaks down, apologising and explaining that joining the gang was his way of coping with the overwhelming grief of losing his father — he did not know how else to process the pain. The scene ends with the mother embracing her son, promising to get through it together, and committing to starting therapy as a family.
fields.tools_used: Nano Banana, AI Image Generation, AI Video Generation, LLM Prompt Engineering
fields.outcome:
Produced a 21-second AI-generated short film with a complete emotional narrative arc — confrontation, breakdown, apology, and reconciliation — entirely from text prompts and AI-generated image ingredients.

Key outcomes:
- Demonstrated end-to-end AI video production: concept → character design → prompt engineering → video generation
- Photorealistic character and environment created without any real footage or actors
- Prompt engineering techniques used to direct emotion, camera movement, and dialogue pacing
- Scene extended beyond the initial generation to complete the narrative with the mother-son hug
- Showcases practical understanding of generative AI tools for storytelling and creative production
fields.business_problem: Traditional video production requires actors, sets, cameras, and editing software — resources that are costly and time-consuming. This project explored whether a complete, emotionally resonant short film could be produced entirely through AI tools, using only text prompts and a generative video platform, to demonstrate the practical capabilities and creative potential of modern AI video generation.
fields.key_features:
AI character and scene design using Nano Banana image generation from detailed text prompts
LLM-assisted prompt engineering to develop shot-by-shot video direction with emotion and camera guidance
AI video generation producing a 21-second cinematic clip from image ingredients and scene prompts
Scene extension using the Extend feature to complete the emotional arc through reconciliation
Full narrative arc delivered: confrontation, breakdown, apology, and mother-son hug
fields.role_contribution: Developed the original scene concept and narrative arc. Wrote all character, environment, and emotion prompts for AI image generation. Used an LLM to refine and expand the scene into shot-by-shot video direction prompts. Directed the AI video generation pipeline through Nano Banana and used the Extend feature to complete the narrative. Responsible for the full creative and production process from concept to final clip.
fields.biggest_challenge:
- Translating an emotionally complex story into effective AI prompts capable of generating believable character expressions, tone, and emotional pacing.
- Maintaining visual consistency across characters, environments, lighting, and camera angles throughout multiple generated scenes.
- Directing AI-generated cinematic elements such as facial emotion, body language, camera movement, and scene transitions using only prompt engineering techniques.
- Managing limitations of generative video tools, including inconsistent outputs, scene distortion, and loss of character continuity between clips.
- Balancing realism and emotional storytelling within a very short 21-second runtime while still delivering a complete narrative arc.
- Extending the generated sequence beyond the original output to include the reconciliation and emotional closing scene without breaking continuity.
fields.what_you_learned:
- How generative AI tools can be used for end-to-end creative storytelling and video production workflows.
- Practical experience with prompt engineering for emotional storytelling, cinematic direction, and character-driven scene generation.
- How to use descriptive prompting to influence camera movement, facial expressions, environment design, lighting, and emotional tone.
- Better understanding of the strengths and limitations of current AI video generation technology.
- Experience maintaining narrative and visual consistency across multiple AI-generated scenes.
- How iterative prompting and refinement significantly improve AI-generated creative outputs.
- Improved storytelling skills by learning how to structure emotional arcs, pacing, and scene progression within short-form video content.
- Hands-on experience creating photorealistic AI-generated characters and cinematic environments without traditional filming resources.

fields.title: Virtual Assistant Creation using Chatbase
fields.short_description: AI-powered virtual assistant built on Chatbase, trained on custom knowledge to answer questions about Rosemary's work and background.
fields.description: Designed and deployed a conversational AI virtual assistant using Chatbase — a no-code/low-code AI platform. The assistant, named Rosemary's Virtual Assistant, was trained on custom knowledge sources covering professional background, skills, projects, and certifications. The chatbot is capable of handling natural language queries from visitors, providing instant, accurate responses about experience and expertise. Configured the agent's persona, response style, and knowledge base entirely within the Chatbase platform.
fields.tools_used: Chatbase, AI/LLM, Prompt Engineering, Knowledge Base Configuration
fields.outcome: Deployed a fully functional AI assistant capable of answering visitor questions about professional background, projects, and skills — with no backend code required.
fields.business_problem: Portfolio visitors had no quick way to get answers about professional background, project experience, or skills without reading through every page. A conversational assistant would allow visitors to ask natural-language questions and receive instant, accurate responses — improving engagement and making the portfolio more interactive and accessible.
fields.key_features:
Custom knowledge base trained on professional background, skills, projects, and certifications
Natural language query handling for visitor questions
Configured assistant persona and response style
Instant, accurate responses without requiring visitors to navigate multiple pages
Deployed via Chatbase's no-code platform with no backend development required
fields.role_contribution: Designed the assistant's persona and tone of voice. Curated and structured the knowledge base content covering professional history, certifications, and project portfolio. Configured the chatbot settings, response style, and fallback behaviour entirely within the Chatbase platform. Embedded the assistant into the portfolio for live visitor use.
fields.biggest_challenge:
- Structuring professional experience, projects, certifications, and technical skills into a clean knowledge base that the AI assistant could understand and retrieve accurately.
- Ensuring the chatbot returned relevant and context-aware answers instead of generic or incomplete responses.
- Refining the assistant’s persona, tone, and response style so interactions felt professional, natural, and aligned with the portfolio brand.
- Managing knowledge base quality to reduce inaccurate responses or hallucinations from incomplete training information.
- Organizing large amounts of portfolio content into categories that improved retrieval accuracy and user experience.
- Testing different prompt configurations and response instructions to improve conversational consistency.
fields.what_you_learned:
- How conversational AI assistants can improve user engagement and accessibility on portfolio and business websites.
- Practical experience building and deploying AI-powered assistants using Chatbase without requiring backend development.
- How retrieval-based AI assistants depend heavily on knowledge base structure and content quality.
- The importance of prompt configuration, persona design, and response instructions in shaping AI behavior.
- How to organize professional and technical information into searchable, AI-friendly formats.

fields.title: Machine Learning: Classification & Regression Models
fields.short_description: Five scikit-learn models solving classification and regression problems across handwritten digits, California housing, Auto MPG, Iris species, and diabetes progression datasets.
fields.description:
This project demonstrates a complete machine learning workflow applied across five datasets, covering both supervised classification and regression tasks using Python and scikit-learn.

ML1 — Digit Classification (KNN): Applied K-Nearest Neighbors to the Digits dataset (1,797 grayscale 8×8 images) to classify handwritten digits 0–9. Achieved ~99% accuracy with a confusion matrix confirming near-perfect classification across all ten classes.

ML2 — California Housing Price Prediction (Linear Regression): Used the California Housing dataset to predict median house values from socioeconomic features including income, house age, average occupancy, and geolocation. Explored feature relationships through scatter plots of HouseAge, AveOccup, Latitude, and Longitude vs MedHouseVal.

ML3 — Auto MPG Fuel Efficiency Prediction (Linear Regression): Predicted vehicle fuel efficiency (MPG) from automobile attributes including weight, horsepower, cylinders, and model year using the Auto MPG dataset. Visualised the strong negative correlation between vehicle weight and MPG.

Iris Species Classification (KNN): Classified three flower species from petal and sepal measurements using the classic 150-sample Iris dataset.

Diabetes Progression Prediction (Linear Regression): Predicted disease progression scores from patient health metrics using the scikit-learn Diabetes dataset.
fields.tools_used: Python, scikit-learn, pandas, NumPy, matplotlib, seaborn, Hugging Face datasets, Jupyter Notebook
fields.outcome:
Successfully trained and evaluated five machine learning models across classification and regression tasks:
- Digit classifier achieved ~99% accuracy with minimal misclassification across all 10 classes
- California Housing and Auto MPG regression models produced predictions closely aligned with actual values
- Iris species classifier achieved high classification accuracy
- Diabetes regression model demonstrated reliable continuous value prediction
- All models validated through appropriate evaluation metrics: accuracy scores, confusion matrices, and actual vs predicted scatter plots
fields.business_problem: Healthcare, real estate, and automotive industries generate large volumes of structured data but often lack automated tools to extract predictive insights. This project addresses how supervised machine learning  both classification and regression  can be applied to real-world datasets to automate predictions, identify patterns, and support data-driven decision-making across multiple domains.
fields.key_features:
Digit classification using K-Nearest Neighbors on 1,797 image samples — ~99% accuracy
Confusion matrix visualisation confirming classification performance across all 10 digit classes
California housing price prediction using Linear Regression with feature relationship scatter plots
Auto MPG fuel efficiency prediction with Weight vs MPG trend visualisation
Iris species classification and Diabetes progression regression
Full ML pipeline: data loading, EDA, preprocessing, train-test split, model training, prediction, and evaluation
Datasets sourced from scikit-learn built-ins and Hugging Face
fields.role_contribution: Sole developer responsible for the full machine learning workflow across all five models. Loaded and explored each dataset, performed exploratory data analysis with matplotlib and seaborn visualisations, preprocessed features (handling missing values, removing non-numeric columns), implemented train-test splits, trained KNN and Linear Regression models using scikit-learn, generated predictions, and evaluated model performance using accuracy scores, confusion matrices, and scatter plots.
fields.biggest_challenge:
- Understanding how to apply different machine learning approaches appropriately across both classification and regression problems.
- Cleaning, exploring, and interpreting structured datasets with different feature types, scales, and distributions.
- Selecting the right algorithms for each task, including when to use K-Nearest Neighbors versus Linear Regression.
- Managing feature relationships and understanding how variables influence predictions and model performance.
- Interpreting model evaluation metrics such as accuracy scores, confusion matrices, and regression prediction plots.
- Handling overfitting and ensuring models generalized well beyond the training data.
- Visualizing datasets effectively to identify trends, correlations, and potential data quality issues.
- Understanding how feature scaling and preprocessing affect distance-based algorithms such as KNN.
- Comparing actual versus predicted values to evaluate regression model reliability and prediction quality.
fields.what_you_learned:
- How to build complete machine learning workflows using Python and scikit-learn.
- Practical understanding of supervised learning concepts including classification and regression.
- How K-Nearest Neighbors works for classification problems such as digit recognition and species identification.
- How Linear Regression can be used to predict continuous outcomes such as housing prices, fuel efficiency, and disease progression.
- Experience splitting datasets into training and testing sets to evaluate model performance objectively.
- Improved understanding of feature engineering, feature relationships, and the impact of variables on predictive accuracy.
- Hands-on experience visualizing data using scatter plots, confusion matrices, and prediction comparison charts.
- How to evaluate classification models using accuracy metrics and confusion matrices.
- How to evaluate regression models by comparing predicted values against actual outcomes and analyzing prediction trends.
